hero.question: Pistons vs. Hornets
hero.prob_take:
At 35.5%, the market is pricing the Pistons as a heavy underdog despite Detroit being competitive enough to warrant closer to 40-42% odds in a road matchup against a struggling Hornets squad.

The 11.5-point slide in 24 hours suggests sharp money fled after injury news or a line move in the sportsbooks, but the selling has gone too far if the Pistons' roster health is actually intact.

daily_take.headline: Pistons-Hornets Collapse Signals Sharp Money Fleeing Detroit
daily_take.deck: Pistons dropped 11.5 points in 24 hours to 35.5%, a brutal signal that smart money just spotted something the market missed. Either Detroit's injury report got worse, or the move is overcorrection from retail panic. Check the Vegas line before you fade this, because a double-digit point swing on $845K volume screams volatility, not conviction. Sharp bettors should verify the injury status before chasing either side.

sidebar.headline: Belmont's Peru bet just spiked 14 points in 24 hours, flagging either new polling or insider movement ahead of the election cycle.
